title: Spicy Shattering Korean Pork Stew
description: A peppery and spicy Korean-inspired stew made with gochugaru and featuring a unique shattering texture, this dish is a quick-fix American adaptation that incorporates ayurvedic-tamasic friendly principles and is cooked in a hot-pot vessel, the mushimono-steam method adds a delicate touch to the ingredients.
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together gochugaru, soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic, and ginger to make the spice paste.
Heat the sesame oil in the hot-pot vessel over medium heat.
Add the pork to the vessel and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the spice paste to the vessel and stir to coat the pork evenly.
Add the potatoes to the vessel and pour in enough water to cover the ingredients.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Stir in the chopped green onions and toasted sesame seeds.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
To achieve the shattering texture, cook the stew for an additional 10 minutes, or until the meat is tender and the potatoes are easily broken with a spoon.
